chats.text: wait is anyone else's laptop overheating rn? like actually hot to the touch
they're all doing it. the 'overheating' is just a way to get us out of the room early without HR asking questions. I saw them testing mics with actual microwaves.
did you notice how they kept saying 'construct' instead of like... building? it's not a typo. i was made aware this is code for something going wrong in the org.

the intern handbook says nothing about bandwidth being measured in feelings. so when they started asking us to 'allocate emotional throughput,' i thought maybe we were finally getting paid for our soul-work.
the coffee machine in the breakroom just started dispensing lukewarm sludge. they said it was 'calibrating' but i think the server room's on fire upstairs and nobody wants to admit it.
i'm not even sure if my laptop is actually overheating or if they're just making me feel like everything I touch is a liability. either way the fan noise sounds suspiciously like breathing.

there's a sticky note on my desk that says 'remember your bandwidth' but it was written by someone who clearly just spilled coffee on their keyboard. so now i'm drinking lukewarm sludge and feeling guilty about my laptop fan.
i keep looking at the exit sign but every time it feels like it's blinking at me. like, in Morse code that probably translates to 'don't leave yet.'
okay i'm literally sweating through my shirt and the AC is on 'low' but i think that's just because they're trying to save energy so we stay longer
they keep saying 'we're all in this together' but the together part is only for people who haven't already been made aware of how broken this place actually is.
i'm going to go take a 'quick stretch' in the hallway but like, real quick. before they clock me for leaving my post during a strategic planning session.
the 'construct' thing is just a cover story for when someone's actual building burns down. like yesterday in accounting, right? but nobody wants to admit it was the intern's fault.
my badge says 'intern' but i'm pretty sure that just means 'we haven't paid you enough so we can blame you for everything' at this point. am i right?
the coffee machine is now dispensing something that looks like sludge but smells faintly of burnt toast and anxiety. i think the server room fire has finally spread to the cafeteria.
I was made aware that the meeting room temperature is below optimal for productive discourse, right? I've already submitted a bandwidth request for a climate control consultant.
I was made aware that someone already booked the catering for lunch. I just wanted to flag the bandwidth, right?
I was also made aware that this is a demo, so the 'you were made aware' badge on my name tag may not reflect my actual status. Am I clear?
I was also made aware that the meeting is 10 minutes over. Constructively? Or deconstructively? Right.
I was made aware that my name appears first on the attendee list. That is a construct of fairness, right? Please correct me if wrong.
I was made aware of the Zoom link at 2:17 PM yesterday. Just checking my bandwidth right?
{NAME}, I was made aware that you've been the one making all these decisions. What does my salary look like?
